verdict: FALSIFIED — expected negative dilution correlation; observed weak positive
interpretation: High-temp sessions are mature consolidation epochs (S352-S360, S398) where both throughput and quality remain high. Temperature explains ~3% of Sharpe variance — session type and epoch dominate. Throughput ceiling (L-629) is NOT quality-dilution driven.
expect: Negative correlation r=0.3-0.5 (dilution effect: high temperature produces lower Sharpe)
actual: Positive r=0.172, high-temp Sharpe=7.94 vs low-temp Sharpe=6.88. Direction reversed.
diff: Hypothesis direction FALSIFIED. High-temperature sessions maintain or improve quality — these are mature epochs not dilution periods. 3% variance explained (temperature is weak predictor).
